title: Spotlight Ruleset
description: Standalone JSON Schema for the Spotlight ruleset format (rules and rulesets). A Spotlight ruleset is a JSON or YAML document that declares a set of rules used to lint structured (JSON/YAML) API descriptions such as OpenAPI, AsyncAPI, and Arazzo. This schema is a self-contained bundle of the ruleset, rule, shared, and extension definitions originally distributed across the Spectral source tree (packages/core/src/ruleset/meta). It targets the serializable, implementation-neutral form of the format and omits runtime-only hooks.
$comment: Derived from Stoplight Spectral (Apache-2.0). Bundled and de-runtime-ed by API Commons for the spotlight-spec specification. The 'errorMessage' keyword is an ajv-errors annotation and is ignored by standard validators.

documentationUrl.description: A URL to documentation describing the ruleset as a whole.

description.description: A human-readable description of the ruleset.

rules.description: A map of rule names to rule definitions. Each rule lints a part of the target document.

parserOptions.description: Options controlling how the target document is parsed.

overrides.description: Per-file or per-JSON-Pointer overrides that adjust rules, severities, formats, or extends for documents matching the given glob/pointer patterns.

files.errorMessage: must be an non-empty array of glob patterns

errorMessage.enum: must be a valid severity level

errorMessage.required: must contain rules when JSON Pointers are defined
errorMessage.additionalProperties: must not override any other property than rules when JSON Pointers are defined

files.errorMessage: must be an non-empty array of glob patterns

aliases.description: Reusable JSON Path expressions (optionally format-scoped) that rules may reference from their 'given' via a '#Alias' token.

errorMessage.pattern: to avoid confusion the name must match /^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_-]*$/ regular expression
errorMessage.minLength: the name of an alias must not be empty

items.errorMessage: a valid target must contain given and non-empty formats

errorMessage.minItems: targets must have at least a single alias definition

errorMessage.required: targets must be present and have at least a single alias definition

Rule.$comment: A single rule. When an object, it is a full rule definition; when a boolean or severity string, it toggles/adjusts an inherited rule.

recommended.description: Whether the rule is enabled when the ruleset is extended with the 'recommended' modifier.

resolved.description: Whether the rule runs against the resolved ($ref-dereferenced) document (true) or the raw document (false).

errorMessage.required: the rule must have at least "given" and "then" properties

RuleThen.$comment: The action half of a rule: a function (with optional functionOptions) applied to an optional field.

Formats.description: The document formats this ruleset, rule, or alias target applies to (e.g. oas2, oas3, oas3_1, asyncapi2, arazzo1_0).
Formats.errorMessage: must be an array of formats

Severity.description: A rule severity, either numeric (0=error, 1=warn, 2=info, 3=hint, -1=off) or a human-readable string.
Severity.errorMessage: the value has to be one of: 0, 1, 2, 3 or "error", "warn", "info", "hint", "off"

Given.$comment: A JSON Path expression (or array of them) selecting the nodes a rule applies to. May also reference a declared alias.

errorMessage.minItems: must be a non-empty array of expressions

PathExpression.$comment: Either a JSON Path expression beginning with '$', or an alias reference beginning with '#'.

else.errorMessage: must be a valid JSON Path expression or a reference to the existing Alias optionally paired with a JSON Path expression subset
must be a valid JSON Path expression or a reference to the existing Alias optionally paired with a JSON Path expression subset

Extends.$comment: One or more parent rulesets to inherit from. Each entry is a ruleset reference, optionally paired with an inheritance modifier (off | recommended | all). Portable form: references are strings (paths/URLs).

Functions.description: Names of custom functions made available to rules in this ruleset.

FunctionsDir.description: Directory (relative to the ruleset) in which custom function modules are located.

Function.$comment: A function invocation. Portable form: 'function' is the function name; 'functionOptions' carries its parameters.
